name: Queen's Gambit
description: Despite the name, no material is truly sacrificed. White offers the c4 pawn temporarily to deflect Black's d5 pawn and establish a dominant central pawn duo. The QGD leads to deep positional play where small advantages accumulate.

moves.instruction: Open the queen's pawn, controlling the center and freeing the c1-bishop.
Mirror White, contesting the center symmetrically.
The Queen's Gambit. Offer a pawn to deflect d5 and build a pawn majority.
The Queen's Gambit Declined. Support d5 and develop, but lock in the c8-bishop.
Develop and put more pressure on the d5-pawn.
Develop the knight, attacking e4 and reinforcing d5.
Pin the knight to the queen, adding a third attacker to d5.
Unpin and develop. Black would otherwise lose the right to castle.
Reinforce d4 and open lines for development and castling.
Castle, completing kingside development and bringing the rook to the center.
Develop the last minor piece and add control over e5 and d4.
Question the bishop. Black wants to play ...Ne4 without losing a piece.
Retreat but keep the pin. White maintains tension rather than releasing it.
Prepare to develop the problem c8-bishop along the long diagonal (b7-a8).
Open the c-file. White removes Black's central pawn to gain queenside pressure.
Recapture toward the center. The classical QGD main tabiya is reached.
